sections.paragraph: m. pl. ἅπαξ λεγόμ. Psa 68:32, “those who are fat,” i.e. rich, nobles; compare Arabic حَشِيمُ, a great man with a large retinue. Well explained by the Hebrew interpreters, אנשים גדולים ונגידים. More far-fetched and improbable is the opinion of Ewald (Hebrew Gram. p. 520) that this word comes from the Arabic خشم the nose, which may be applied to a prince, like اذف a nose, a prince. Indeed, the Arabic Lexicons do not acknowledge such a noun as خشم signifying nose, although حُشَامُ is a large-nosed man, خشم to break the cartilages of the nose. Compare under חַרְטֹם.
